sources_exist.note: All seven canonical URLs resolved to the cited documents or pages. LOW_CONFIDENCE is substantively warranted because the record itself acknowledges thin code-specific research and severe 4-digit role-mix contamination.

figures_match_sources.note: Three citations support their narrow quoted benchmarks (IAB 30% aggregate full-scale adoption, Axial agency multiple ranges, and Oberlo's 37% age-55-to-73 share), but four score-affecting citations do not support the claims made for NAICS 541840. The ESTIMATE_HEAVY flag is unresolved: active_consolidators, exit_mult, and pricing_structure lack directly comparable support, while owners_60plus_pct extrapolates a non-60+ generic population.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: Declared role fractions, pi_dist=0.45, pi_moat=0.25, and t50_years=4.0 are bounded and directionally plausible for relationship sales exposed to programmatic bypass; the role shares sum to 1 and contributions sum to 0.381. This does not cure unsupported score-affecting research inputs.

rubric_consistent.note: The BORDERLINE flag (|S - pass cut 3.00| = 0.0102) is unresolved because S is highly sensitive to unsupported buy/exit multiple and consolidator inputs. MELTING and LOW confidence are applied consistently with the frozen caps, but a borderline result cannot be accepted when its M and B evidence is non-comparable or absent.

cross_checks_honest.note: Uplift, terminal-value, and no-proxy checks are candid, but pricing-model deflation repeats an unsupported 10-15% media-representative commission premise from a survey of advertiser compensation for media planning/buying agencies. The cited survey instead shows predominantly labor/fixed fees and much lower traditional-media commission brackets.

source_audits.note: The page is a 2026 general agency/marketing M&A article, reports adtech/marketing-services activity up 13% over 2024, and contains no media-representative or NAICS 541840 analysis. It cannot establish one active code-specific consolidator or the quoted 14% YTD 2025 figure.

The page reports 2.5x-3.9x for agencies under $1M EBITDA, 3.5x-5x for $1M-plus, and 3.33x average for agencies around $500k adjusted EBITDA. It supports the general-agency benchmark and the record transparently labels 3.5x as an ESTIMATE adjusted for media-rep decline, though it does not provide code-specific transactions.

The IAB report states 70% of agencies, brands, and publishers had not fully scaled AI across media planning, activation, and analysis, implying 30% aggregate full-scale deployment (n=529). This is an aggregate media-campaign benchmark, not a 541840-specific estimate, as the record acknowledges.

The table gives point averages by agency type and EBITDA band: for $3-5M EBITDA, 6.1x digital, 7.6x advertising, and 8.2x traditional marketing; for $5-10M, 9x, 9.5x, and 10.4x respectively. It does not report the quoted 6.1x-8.2x as a size range, a 3.5x-5.5x smaller-agency range, or support a 5.5x $10M-plus media-representative platform exit.

The 2014 article supports about $10B and 45% of US digital-display spend in 2014 and a forecast above $20B by 2016. It does not contain the claimed 61% global figure for 2018, document a 2011-2012 10% baseline, or establish a measured four-to-five-year time to 50% adoption.

The page reports that 37% of generic US small-business owners in 2024 were ages 55-73. The record marks its 38% age-60-plus value as an ESTIMATE and explicitly discloses the population and age-threshold mismatch plus lack of a 541840 succession study.

This ANA survey concerns advertiser compensation of media planning and buying agencies, not media representatives selling inventory for media owners. It says over 70% use labor-based or fixed fees for planning/offline buying, only 12% use commissions for traditional buying, and surveyed traditional-media commission brackets mostly run 1.5%-10%; it does not establish a typical 10-15% gross-billings commission for NAICS 541840.

reasons: At inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://www.feinternational.com/blog/agency-marketing-ma-consolidation-ai-exit-opportunities), replace the general 2026 agency article with dated evidence identifying PE-backed platforms actively completing NAICS 541840 add-ons in the last 24 months, or document a zero/estimate research chain; the cited page cannot support value 1.

At inputs.exit_mult.url (https://firstpagesage.com/business/marketing-agency-valuation-multiples-and-valuations/), supply recent $10M-plus EBITDA media-representative transactions or explicitly normalized credible comps. The current table neither reports the quoted ranges nor supports a 5.5x platform exit for this code.

At inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://martech.org/us-programmatic-ad-spend-10-billion-2014-double-2016-emarketer/), provide evidence for both the analog's starting adoption share and actual crossing of 50%, with consistent geography, denominator, and dates; the cited article only establishes 45% of US digital-display spend in 2014 and forecasts.

At inputs.pricing_structure.url (https://s3.amazonaws.com/media.mediapost.com/uploads/MediaAgencyCompensation.pdf), use evidence about third-party media representatives' compensation from represented media owners. The current media-buying-agency survey contradicts the asserted typical 10-15% commission mix and makes the pi_dist and pricing-deflation cross-check unsupported.
flags_reviewed: ESTIMATE_HEAVY: 4 inputs with quality ESTIMATE (active_consolidators, buy_mult, exit_mult, owners_60plus_pct)
